finding: Export + copy paths both remain formula-neutralized (rowsToDelimited/neutralizeFormula for the whole view; kit sanitizeClipboard for selectionTsv); dev-only fake gallery File has no untrusted DOM sink.

TabularViewState/setFileTabularView consistent; optional TabularToolbar props keep the XlsxSheet caller valid; DelimitedTable fileId optional so inline chat no-ops; fake gallery file satisfies FileEntity.
Both new icon-buttons carry matching aria-label + tooltip, ghost/icon variant, disabled-removes-from-tab-order and default tooltip side — satisfies the gallery WCAG-name gate.
publishView per-call cost is cheap even for large tables — rows stored by reference (no copy), the copied Map holds ~1 entry per open file, activeColumns is O(columns).
Mount/data effect published viewRef.current, which is stale on a text/delimiter change until the kit's onViewChange re-fires — briefly carrying the previous file's rows under the new fileId.
resolution: F1: the data effect now resets viewRef/visibleKeysRef/selectionRef to the fresh dataSource before publishView.

finding: onExport silently no-ops when the snapshot is absent, and exportTabularView/downloadBlob has no try/catch so a Blob/download failure is swallowed without a toast.
resolution: F7: onExport wrapped in try/catch -> 'Failed to export' toast; the button is disabled when no snapshot exists.

finding: fileTabularView is evicted only in onFileSync/onReconnect, never on DelimitedTable unmount, so the entry lingers after the table body unmounts (raw view / panel close), leaving the header actions enabled on a stale snapshot.
resolution: F2: added clearFileTabularView + a DelimitedTable unmount cleanup effect.

finding: (dup of state-management) snapshot never cleared on unmount, header actions act on a no-longer-rendered view.
Export-view used the same Download glyph as the shell-level DownloadButton rendered beside it, yielding two adjacent download-icon buttons with different meanings.
resolution: F5: Export-view now uses the FileDown glyph, distinct from the shell's Download tray.

finding: Copy-selection silently copied the ENTIRE view when nothing was selected, diverging from chrome.tsx CopySelectionButton (which warns and copies nothing).
resolution: F3: removed the whole-view fallback; the header warns 'Select a cell to copy' and copies nothing on an empty selection.

finding: testid file-viewer-tabular-copy-btn named a 'Copy selection' action, inconsistent with chrome.tsx's file-viewer-copy-selection-btn.
resolution: F8: renamed to file-viewer-tabular-copy-selection-btn (spec + generated registry updated).

finding: The new Copy-selection button toasted 'Copied to clipboard' while the sibling CopySelectionButton in chrome.tsx toasts 'Copied selection' — inconsistent wording for the same affordance.
resolution: F4: success toast is now 'Copied selection'.

finding: 'Export view' tooltip dropped the CSV/TSV format specificity the removed toolbar labels ('Export CSV'/'Export TSV') carried.
resolution: F6: tooltip/aria-label is now 'Export view (CSV)'/'Export view (TSV)', derived from the snapshot delimiter.

finding: Retargeted TEST-23/25 preserve the original coverage, but the export-honors-hidden-columns path (visibleKeysRef -> exportTabularView) was only exercised indirectly.
resolution: Added a unit test locking rowsToDelimited over the visible-column subset (the path exportTabularView relies on).

finding: The disabled-until-publish state has no test.
resolution: The body publishes synchronously on mount, so the disabled window is a transient mount detail not deterministically observable in e2e — a test would be flaky. The enabled/functional path is covered by TEST-23/25.

finding: copyTabularSelection's clipboard-failure catch is untested.
resolution: navigator.clipboard failure is not reliably inducible in headless browser tests; the catch mirrors the pre-existing MermaidBlock / kit-table copy pattern.

finding: The header re-renders on every publish (new Map + new snapshot object) though hasView stays true after the first publish.
resolution: Idiomatic reactive read; two trivial icon buttons; the read is also needed to retitle the export label from the snapshot delimiter. Negligible cost.

finding: Header subscribes to the whole fileTabularView Map, re-rendering on any file's publish.
resolution: Same as perf; a per-file boolean selector would add complexity for no measurable gain; two-button re-render is negligible.

finding: publishView runs on every onSelectionChange -> a store write per selection change during rapid drag-selection.
resolution: Selection changes are user-paced; a single Map copy + object per change is negligible; no measured churn.

finding: On mount/data change the snapshot is published twice (kit onViewChange + the data effect).
resolution: The data effect is the authoritative fresh-parse publish (F1) and guarantees correctness regardless of kit effect ordering; the extra idempotent write is negligible.
